rules.description: All contributions must use the Standard workflow: fork → branch → commit → PR.
No uncommitted WIP reaching main. All contributions must be complete and intentional.
Sensitive configuration must never be committed to source control in plain text.
Branch names must follow: users/[username]/[CATEGORY]-[entity]-[action].
[username] must be the GitHub username of the contributor.
[CATEGORY] must be from the Standard category list.
[entity] must identify the entity or resource being worked on.
[action] must describe the specific action being performed.
Branch name parts must be separated by / between users/username/ and the category block.

Branch [action] must use the language appropriate to the layer: broker branches use infrastructure verbs (insert, select-all, select-by-id, update, delete); service branches use business verbs (add, retrieve-all, retrieve-by-id, modify, remove). Never mix layers — a BROKERS branch must not use 'add' or 'retrieve'.
One branch represents one operation. When a prompt is ambiguous about scope, the agent must ask which specific operation to implement before creating the branch or generating any code.
Category must accurately reflect the type of work being done.
Size suffix reflects test count: MAJOR=5+, MEDIUM=3-4, MINOR=1-2.
When unsure between MAJOR/MEDIUM/MINOR, choose based on tests added or modified.
Every TDD commit follows FAIL/PASS discipline.
FAIL commit: test written and verified to fail. Prefix: [FAIL].
PASS commit: implementation written, all tests passing. Prefix: [PASS].
Never commit a FAIL without running and confirming the test is red.
Never commit a PASS without running the full suite and confirming all green.
FAIL and PASS commits must be atomic — one test per FAIL, one implementation unit per PASS.
PR titles must include the Standard category: [CATEGORY]: [Entity] - [Description].
PRs must not include sensitive data in the diff.
PRs must show passing CI (build + tests) before merge.
A human reviewer must approve before merging. AI may suggest but not merge.
PRs should be focused on one category — avoid mixing FEATURE + INFRA.
Never hard-code environment-specific values in source code.
Use environment variables or secrets management for sensitive values.
Local development configuration files must be in .gitignore.
GitHub Actions must use repository secrets, not plain text values.
Configuration model classes must not contain default hard-coded production values.
Open-source contributions must use the forking workflow.
Contributors must never push directly to the official repository (unless maintainer).
PRs from forks must be reviewed and build-verified before merge.
